checks.a_analysis: The output JSON accurately reflects the text content found in the source PDF. No fabricated paragraphs, sentences, or sections were identified. The structure follows the visual flow of the three-page presentation.

The contact emails provided on page 3 of the source document ('katie.doherty@rutherfordcountync.gov', 'relkins@sosnc.gov', and 'elizabeth.daniel@nc.gov') are all correctly captured in the output JSON without modification.
The output contains sections corresponding to all three pages of the source document: the title page, the map page, and the final contact page.
There are no tables in the source document or the output JSON.

checks.a_analysis: All critical text, including titles, dates, source information, and contact details, has been preserved in the output. The legend information from the map on page 2 is appropriately captured within the image alt text.

checks.a_analysis: The text in the output matches the source document. Wording and dates ('October 7, 2025') are identical. Formatting like italics for the date on page 1 is correctly noted in the JSON.

checks.a_analysis: Each content block in the output matches a unique block in the source document. While the date 'October 7, 2025' appears in both the first and second sections, this accurately reflects its intentional repetition on both the cover page and the map page of the source PDF. No erroneous duplication of paragraphs, lists, or sections was found.

The output maintains a logical heading structure. The main document title on page 1 is captured as the title of the first section, and the subsequent page titles are captured as titles for sections 2 and 3. This represents a flat, normalized hierarchy suitable for screen reader navigation.
The source document contains no tables; accordingly, the output contains no table elements. There is no corruption of tabular data.

The reading order in the JSON output follows the logical progression of the source document page by page and element by element (Heading -> Date -> Body/Images). The legend data from the map on page 2 is appropriately integrated into the image's alt text for accessibility, preserving the flow of information.
The source document does not contain formatted lists. The 'Sources' section on page 2 is a block of multi-line text, which the output correctly represents as a paragraph element.
The output correctly identifies and provides mailto links for the three contact email addresses listed on page 3. No links were hallucinated, and no visible links in the source were missed.

checks.a_analysis: The HTML output maintains the visual organization and flow of information from the source PDF. The transformation from a multi-column or split-screen presentation (like PDF page 1) to a single-column vertical stack is a standard and acceptable practice for responsive HTML conversion. The information sequence (Title -> Graphic -> Map -> Contact Information) is preserved. While the 'Sources' text on Page 2 has moved from below the map to above it in the HTML, the overall visual organization remains intuitive and clear.

The source document does not contain any formal data tables. The legend and project status information on Page 2 are part of a complex graphic and are rendered correctly within the map image in the HTML output.

All meaningful visual assets from the source PDF are present and correctly rendered in the HTML. This includes the large NCDIT graphic from the cover, the comprehensive map graphic from Page 2, and the NCGICC and NCDIT logos. No meaningful images are missing or appear as broken links.

The content from PDF Page 2 is visually duplicated in the HTML output. The title 'NC Parcels Project Status by County', the date 'October 7, 2025', and the entire 'Sources' paragraph are rendered first as HTML text elements and then appear again immediately below inside the map image (which is a screenshot of the entire PDF page). This results in a redundant presentation of identical body content blocks.
checks.b_evidence_source: PDF Page 2 contains the title 'NC Parcels Project Status by County', the date 'October 7, 2025', and the 'Sources' paragraph at the bottom left.
checks.b_evidence_output: The HTML renders 'NC Parcels Project Status by County', 'October 7, 2025', and the full 'Sources' paragraph as text, and then displays them a second time within the boundaries of the following map image.
checks.b_evidence_location: Middle section of the HTML document, corresponding to Page 2 of the source PDF.
